name: Vitigudino to Castro de Yecla la Vieja Loop
name_original: Castro Vetón de Yecla la Vieja – circular desde Vitigudino
description: This moderate 19.7-mile mountain biking route from Vitigudino takes you to the ancient Vettonian Hillfort of Yecla la Vieja.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Vitigudino offers diverse natural landscapes within the province of Salamanca, western Spain. The region is characterized by its rural charm, vast open spaces, and the dramatic river canyons of the Arribes del Duero Natural Park. Mountain bike trails traverse areas featuring impressive rock formations, the unique dehesa ecosystem, and varied terrain suitable for cycling. The landscape provides a mix of open country and natural features for exploration. Best mountain bike trails around Vitigudino The most popular mountain bike trail is Vitigudino to Castro de Yecla la Vieja Loop, a 19.7 miles (31.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 18 minutes to complete. This route explores the rural surroundings and leads towards the historic Castro de Yecla la Vieja. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Vettonian Hillfort of Yecla la Vieja – Vettonian Hillfort of Yecla la Vieja loop from Vitigudino, a moderate 26.3 miles (42.4 km) path. This trail offers views of the ancient hillfort and the surrounding natural environment. Local mountain bikers also love the Old Rio Yeltes Mill – Vettonian Hillfort of Yecla la Vieja loop from Vitigudino, a 36.0 miles (58.0 km) trail leading through the scenic Rio Yeltes area, often completed in about 4 hours 30 minutes. Mountain biking around Vitigudino is defined by rural landscapes, river valleys, and the dehesa ecosystem, with options for different ability levels. The routes in Vitigudino are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 4 reviews. More than 18 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Vitigudino's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Vitigudino?
A: There are 7 mountain bike trails available around Vitigudino on komoot. These routes offer a variety of experiences within the region's diverse natural landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy or difficult mountain bike trails in Vitigudino?
A: All 7 mountain bike trails listed for Vitigudino are rated as moderate. There are currently no trails classified as easy or difficult, suggesting a consistent level of challenge across the available routes.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while mountain biking near Vitigudino?
A: Mountain biking around Vitigudino offers a mix of rural charm, vast open spaces, and the dramatic river canyons of the Arribes del Duero Natural Park. You'll traverse areas featuring impressive rock formations, the unique dehesa ecosystem, and varied terrain, providing a rich natural environment to explore.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Vitigudino?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Vitigudino are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994050340/vitigudino-to-castro-de-yecla-la-vieja-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vitigudino to Castro de Yecla la Vieja Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular trail exploring rural surroundings and leading to the historic Castro de Yecla la Vieja.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions I can see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many routes pass by significant points of interest. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38151253" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vettonian Hillfort of Yecla la Vieja – Vettonian Hillfort of Yecla la Vieja loop from Vitigudino&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5446366" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vettonian Hillfort of Yecla la Vieja&lt;/a&gt;. The region also boasts natural viewpoints like Peña La Vela and the impressive Pozo de los Humos waterfall in the Arribes del Duero Natural Park.

Q: Is Vitigudino suitable for family-friendly mountain biking?
A: While all listed routes are moderate, the region's extensive trail networks offer varied difficulty levels. For families, it's advisable to check individual route descriptions for specific terrain and elevation profiles to ensure they match your family's skill and fitness levels. The rural landscapes and open spaces provide a pleasant environment for cycling.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Vitigudino?
A: The rural and natural environment around Vitigudino is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs under control, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to check local regulations for specific trails or natural parks like Arribes del Duero. Ensure your dog is fit enough for the moderate trails.

Q: When is the best time of year for mountain biking in Vitigudino?
A: The region is excellent for outdoor activities. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking, avoiding the intense heat of summer. During the rainy season, the Pozo de los Humos waterfall is particularly magnificent, adding to the scenic beauty.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the mountain bike trails in Vitigudino?
A: Vitigudino and nearby charming villages like Hinojosa de Duero offer options for rural tourism, including gastronomy and accommodation. These villages are ideal spots for cyclists looking for places to refuel and rest after their rides. The region is known for its local charm and hospitality.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Vitigudino?
A: The mountain bike routes in Vitigudino are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 4 reviews. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, rural landscapes, and the natural beauty of the area, particularly the river valleys and the dehesa ecosystem.

Q: How can I access the trailheads for mountain biking in Vitigudino?
A: Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31813200" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Rio Yeltes Mill – Vettonian Hillfort of Yecla la Vieja loop from Vitigudino&lt;/a&gt;, start directly from Vitigudino itself, making access straightforward. Parking is generally available in and around the town, and the rural nature of the area means trailheads are often easily reachable by car.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Arribes del Duero Natural Park?
A: Yes, the region around Vitigudino is part of the Arribes del Duero Natural Park, known for its dramatic gorges and stunning viewpoints. While specific routes within this guide may not explicitly state they are entirely within the park, the area's extensive trail networks include challenging routes that traverse this spectacular natural feature, offering unparalleled vistas of the Duero River.
tips:
Archaeological site where the ruins of El Castro Castle are found, whose wall is the only remaining vestige and surrounds an area of approximately 5 hectares. Within the property is also the Hermitage of the Virgen del Castillo and tour the place through the various paths to see other remains and some stone engravings of animals and human figures.
It is estimated that the construction dates from the Iron Age.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The Castro Vetón de Yecla la Vieja – circular desde Vitigudino mountain bike route is estimated to take approximately 2 hours and 18 minutes to complete. This duration is for the full 31.7 km (19.7 mi) loop.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain for this route, typical of the Salamanca province, can be diverse. You can expect bridle paths, old olive groves, and rural tracks. Near the historical site, there might be rocky or uneven sections. The route traverses the characteristic dehesa landscape with holm oaks.

faqs.question: What historical sites or natural features will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the impressive Vettonian Hillfort of Yecla la Vieja, an ancient Celtic settlement with well-preserved granite walls and petroglyphs. Inside the Castro, you'll find the 18th-century Ermita de la Virgen del Castillo. The route also offers scenic views of the dehesa landscape and is near the confluence of the Huebra River and Varlaña stream.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is rated as 'moderate' difficulty. While the broader region offers routes for various skill levels, this specific trail may include varying levels of challenge, potentially with some steep slopes or technical sections (known as 'trialeras'). Riders should have some mountain biking experience.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring colder, wetter conditions, making some sections more challenging.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Castro Vetón de Yecla la Vieja mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Since the route is a circular one starting from Vitigudino, you would typically find parking within Vitigudino itself to begin your ride. Specific parking areas for the Castro are not detailed, but local parking in Vitigudino would be the starting point.

faqs.question: Is the Castro Vetón de Yecla la Vieja route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact route are not provided, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through rural areas, near livestock, or within the archaeological site to protect both your pet and the local environment/wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to access the mountain bike route itself or the Vettonian Hillfort of Yecla la Vieja. Access to the archaeological site is generally open to the public.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. You will ride along parts of the Camino de Guadramiro, Camino a Vitigudino, camino a Gema, Camino del Coche, and Camino de Cipérez, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options nearby?
faqs.answer: As the route starts and ends in Vitigudino, you will find various amenities there, including cafes, pubs, and accommodation options. Vitigudino serves as a good base for exploring the surrounding area.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife or plants might I encounter?
faqs.answer: The route traverses the characteristic dehesa landscape, dominated by holm oaks. You might encounter typical Iberian wildlife such as various bird species, rabbits, and potentially deer, especially in the quieter sections away from populated areas.
